sglang.title: [refactor] Config resolution pipeline: full-stack review (10-PR series, review only)
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Full-stack review PR for the 10-PR config-resolution series (continues #30063–#30077). This PR carries the complete diff plus a placeholder file and runs full CI — review and CI only, do not merge; merging happens through the individual stack PRs below.

sglang.title: Fix Diffusion GT generation pipelines
sglang.author: e-martirosian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
PR #28762 removed collect_test_items from run_suite.py, which broke gen_diffusion_ci_outputs.py used by the diffusion-ci-gt-gen*.yml workflows.

Modifications
This change adds collect_test_items directly to gen_diffusion_ci_outputs.py to restore the affected workflows.

Accuracy Tests

Sp

sglang.title: [Diffusion] Diffusion model support log-requests
sglang.author: LLThomas
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Diffusion model serving (image/video generation) did not support --log-requests, --log-requests-level, --log-requests-format, or --log-requests-target, so these flags were rejected at startup. The LLM serving path already had full support via RequestLogger in srt/utils/request_logger.py.

sglang.title: [chore] Remove the stack-review placeholder file
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
STACK_REVIEW_PLACEHOLDER.md existed only to distinguish the full-stack review PR (#30137) from its last stack member; the review PR ended up being the merge vehicle, so the placeholder landed on main with it. This removes it.
🤖 Generated with Claude Code


CI States
Latest PR Test (Base): ✅ Run #287

sglang.title: [diffusion] Clean up duplicate helper definitions
sglang.author: BBuf
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Reuse the shared pytest runner helpers in the diffusion GT generation script instead of redefining collection locally.
Remove duplicate imports from the JoyEcho memory helpers.
Remove duplicate PipelineConfig hook definitions that were shadowed by later definitions.

Tests

sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt: The config-resolution series (#30137) pinned the get_global_server_args exact-pin baseline at 278, and #28787 concurrently added two call sites in layernorm.py (rl_on_policy_target reads on the ROCm batch-invariant path). Both were green against their own merge bases, but the combined tree counts 28

sglang.title: [BugFix] Release HiCache prefetch resources on disagg-prefill bootstrap-queue abort
sglang.author: kflansburg
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When abort_request() processes requests still in disagg_prefill_bootstrap_queue, it aborts the KV sender but never calls release_aborted_request(). Since _add_request_to_queue() calls _prefetch_kvcache() (which increments prefetch_tokens_occupied and creates an ongoing_prefetch entry) bef

sglang.title: [refactor] Reorder ServerArgs sections common-first; inline LLAMA4/MIMO_V2 arch tuples
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The ~39 field sections under ServerArgs had grown into an ad-hoc order (e.g. SSL/TLS was near the top, distributed parallelism was buried, perf/backend knobs were scattered). This PR reorders the sections into a common-first grouping so the most frequently used options appear first:

Core

sglang.title: Remove `# fmt: off` from environ.py Envs class
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The Envs class in python/sglang/srt/environ.py wrapped its entire body in # fmt: off / # fmt: on to keep the env-var registry as a compact one-line-per-entry table. The side effect is that black-normalizations are also suppressed for the whole block.
This PR drops the # fmt: off / # fmt:

sglang.title: Cleanup: relocate temp_set_env and consolidate multi-device/CUDA helpers in common.py
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Two small, behavior-neutral cleanups to python/sglang/srt/utils/common.py and python/sglang/srt/environ.py:


Move temp_set_env out of environ.py. temp_set_env is a general-purpose env helper that explicitly rejects sglang-owned (SGLANG_*/SGL_*) keys, so it doesn't belong in environ.py (w

sglang.title: [AMD] [Docker] Update MoRI to v1.2.1
sglang.author: Duyi-Wang
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
This PR upgrades the MoRI version used by the ROCm Docker image from v1.2.0 to v1.2.1.
Related to #27669, which updated MoRI to v1.2.0. The new v1.2.1 tag includes the corresponding upstream fix for MoRI support on ROCm 7.1.4, so this bump brings that support fix into the SGLang ROCm Docker

Motivation
The DeepSeek-V4 DSA indexer selects the top-k KV positions per query for sparse
attention and maps the selected indices through a page table. This fused

sglang.title: [AMD ]Feat/dsv4 ep tbo prefill
sglang.author: kkHuang-amd
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation from TBO design of rocm/ATOM (https://github.com/ROCm/ATOM)
Add DeepSeek-V4 prefill two-batch-overlap (TBO) together with a set of AMD (HIP,MI355) prefill optimizations. TBO splits a prefill batch into two micro-batches and overlaps one ubatch's MoE communication with the other's attentio

sglang.title: [XPU] Unbreak stage-b: re-add --disable-decode-cuda-graph, quarantine EAGLE3 parity
sglang.author: arathi-hlab
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Re-adds --disable-decode-cuda-graph in test/registered/xpu/test_intel_xpu_backend.py (common_args).
Restores the escape hatch that was originally introduced in #29053 and removed in #29911.
Stops stage-b (stage-b-test-1-gpu-xpu) from timing out on every PR and push-to-main.

Why stage-b is

sglang.title: [Intel XPU] Bump nightly per-file timeout to 120m and extend XPU CI monitor
sglang.author: arathi-hlab
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

nightly-test-intel.yml: bump --timeout-per-file from 1800/2400 → 7200 (120 min) for both nightly-xpu-2-gpu and nightly-xpu-4-gpu. The 4-GPU Qwen3-32B (TP=4) test consistently exceeds 40 min end-to-end (load + 200-example GSM8K eval on 4× Intel Arc Pro B60), and the 2400s cap was firing mid-

sglang.title: [Model] Support Qwen3.6 ModelOpt mixed NVFP4
sglang.author: mmangkad
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Support nvidia/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-NVFP4. Closes #26967.
This checkpoint is a little different from the regular modelopt_fp4 NVFP4 path. Its config.json reports ModelOpt MIXED_PRECISION and lists per-layer FP8 / W4A16 NVFP4 entries under wrapper prefixes like model.language_model.*, including

Add progressive_mode, progressive_levels, and progressi

sglang.title: [diffusion] perf: tp-shard every text/image encoder across the full DiT replica (any parallelism)
sglang.author: mickqian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Encoders (text and image) run in the encoding stage, before denoising, when every GPU in the DiT replica is idle. Yet only the T5 encoder was parallel-folded (TP-sharded), and originally only under pure SP (tp==1 && sp>1). Every other multi-GPU layout, and every non-T5 encoder, left the e

vllm.title: [Bugfix][TurboQuant] Preserve KV cache dtype in backend shape
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Fix TurboQuant KV cache shape setup so TQFullAttentionSpec carries its real cache dtype (for example turboquant_k8v4) into backend shape/layout computation instead of being treated as auto.
This prevents TurboQuant backends from seeing auto and failing with an unknown TurboQuant cache dtype

Support sequence parallel without the need for DP
Originally we must have DP to eanble sequence parallel, but this constraint is not needed, this PR fixes the issue.

vllm.author: Sunt-ing
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Loading a Mamba/Mamba2 checkpoint whose config.json omits the top-level architectures field crashes engine core init with a bare, message-less AssertionError: mamba_block_size is not None. This hits the official HF mamba2 conversions such as AntonV/mamba2-130m-hf and AntonV/mamba2-370m-hf.
f

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Voxtral Realtime] Fix token feedback timeout silent hang
vllm.author: Sunt-ing
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Closes #36015.
Closes #35863.
Voxtral realtime has a background feed_tokens() task that moves engine output tokens from input_stream into the realtime transcription buffer. That task currently wraps input_stream.get() in asyncio.wait_for(..., VLLM_ENGINE_ITERATION_TIMEOUT_S). A normal idle i

vllm.title: [MRV2] Enable mm prefix bidi attention support on MRV2
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

Wire mm prefix attn mask computation to MRV2
Actually, tests/models/multimodal/generation/test_common.py's gemma3 test can't catch the difference of with/without mm_prefixlm attention, because their logprobs are still quite similar.
This PR adds test to cover this regression.

vllm.author: spandantiwari
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
test_per_token_group_quant_fp8 fails consistently on MI355 (gfx950) with rtol=0.15. This is potentially a test tolerance issue, may not be a kernel bug.
Root Cause
The test compares the output of the Triton-based per_token_group_quant_fp8 kernel against a PyTorch reference (native_per_token_

vllm.title: [XPU] Fix CUDA API shims breaking Torch Dynamo during AOT compile
vllm.author: lslusarczyk
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
On XPU, XPUModelRunner applies _torch_cuda_wrapper(), which patches torch.cuda.* to delegate to torch.xpu.*. Previously, callables were assigned by direct alias (e.g. torch.cuda.current_stream = torch.xpu.current_stream), so CUDA and XPU shared the same function object.
During EngineCore sta

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose:
Fix Eagle3 draft model embedding sharing when the target and draft models have different embedding widths.
Why
tests/models/test_initialization.py uses a patched _initialize_kv_caches_v1 to skip the warmup path. That patch works under fork with create_process_for_each_test because the child

vllm.title: [Test][XPU] Skip fork in kv_sharing_fast_prefill test on XPU
vllm.author: Liangliang-Ma
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Pytest has already touched XPU before this decorator runs (via torch.xpu.is_available() in conftest.py), so os.fork() in the fork_new_process_for_each_test decorator triggers 'Cannot re-initialize XPU in forked subprocess'. ROCm bypasses the decorator for the same reason; extend the escape hatch to

vllm.author: mayuyuace
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Default number of max-num-seqs would cause the UT failure on XPU.
Limit it to 64 to avoid device error.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][CPU][RISC-V] Fix VLEN detection for RVV attention path
vllm.author: I3eg1nner
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Three bugs in the RISC-V VLEN detection chain caused the RVV-optimized attention kernel to be silently disabled or the build to fail:


cpu_attn_has_isa("rvv") only checked __riscv_v_min_vlen == 128, excluding VLEN=256 hardware (e.g. Spacemit X100). This is inconsistent with cpu_attn_rvv.hpp

vllm.title: [Performance][Hardware][RISC-V] Reduce LMUL pressure in INT4 LUT dequant
vllm.author: I3eg1nner
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The FP32Vec16(int64_t, FP32Vec16&) LUT constructor extracts 16 four-bit nibble indices from a packed int64 using u64 vectors at LMUL_1024.  Using u64 to process an int64 input is natural — on fixed-width SIMD like x86 AVX, element width has no impact on register file pressure.  However, RVV'

vllm.title: [RISC-V] Enable BF16 on VLEN=256 hardware
vllm.author: velonica0
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
#36578 added find_isa(${CPUINFO} "zvfbfmin" RVV_BF16_FOUND) to detect BF16 support via /proc/cpuinfo.
However, the latest hardware currently available (Spacemit X100 / K3) does not report zvfbfmin in /proc/cpuinfo due to a kernel/firmware limitation, causing BF16 to be silently disabled.
VLL

vllm.title: [Frontend] Support OpenAI Responses API namespace tools
vllm.author: zhongjing123
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes #46737.
Namespace tools are expanded to an internal namespace__tool representation for prompting and parsing, but the Responses API should expose the original tool name expected by clients.
This PR introduces a consistent mapping between the external tool name and the internal namespac
